Wine Name: Sancerre Rouge La Croix du Roy
Description: Elegant and mineral, red cherry and violet, silky tannins. Excellent with salmon or duck.
Crisp citrus and white peach, flinty mineral finish. Ideal BTG for seafood-forward menus.
Classic Sancerre: grapefruit zest, gooseberry, and that distinctive chalky minerality.
Full-bodied with firm tannins, tar and roses, dried cherry. Cellar 3-5 years or decant.

Wine Name: Brunello di Montalcino Pieve Santa Restituta
Description: Concentrated and complex, iron-inflected dark fruit, long age-worthy structure.
Italy's most famous Cabernet. Cassis, graphite, and violet. Outstanding 2019 vintage.
Bright and aromatic, white flowers and citrus peel. Perfect pool-bar pour.
Volcanic finesse, strawberry and wild herbs, translucent ruby. Burgundy-lovers' pick.
The quintessential rosé Champagne. Strawberry mousse, brioche, impeccable balance.

Wine Name: Champagne Blanc de Blancs Grand Cru
Description: Pure Chardonnay from Grand Cru vineyards. Chalk, lemon curd, green apple. Exceptional BTG.
Versatile house Champagne, bright and biscuity. Reliable by-the-glass anchor.

Wine Name: Chablis Premier Cru Montée de Tonnerre
Description: Benchmark Chablis. Oyster shell, flint, white flowers. One of the world's finest whites.
Village-level brilliance: hazelnut, white peach, creamy texture with laser-like acid.
Biodynamic Mâcon from Leflaive. Exceptional entry point to the domaine. Lemon and almond.
Old-vine Gevrey, earthy and structured. Plum, violet, forest floor. Decant 30 min.
Silky tannins, cassis and eucalyptus, polished oak. Classic Stag's Leap style.
Restrained and precise, Burgundian in spirit. Citrus oil, toasted brioche, saline finish.
Iconic RRV Pinot. Dark cherry, cola, and forest floor. Velvety texture, long finish.
Rhône-inspired, co-fermented with Viognier. Bacon fat, blackberry, white pepper.
Tropical and lively, grapefruit and passionfruit, hint of oak. BTG crowd-pleaser.
100% Tempranillo from Vega-Sicilia's Alion estate. Dark fruit and mocha, beautifully integrated.
Mineral and powerful, slate-driven terroir, black olive and dark plum. Bold pairing for lamb.

Wine Name: Rías Baixas Albariño Pazo de Señoráns
Description: Benchmark Albariño. Peach, apricot, and citrus zest with bright acidity. Perfect seafood wine.
Smaragd-level intensity. White pepper, grapefruit, and minerals. Austria's greatest Grüner.
Off-dry, aromatic, delicate slate minerality. Only 8% alcohol — brilliant BTG option.

Wine Name: Zind-Humbrecht Riesling Rangen de Thann Clos Saint Urbain
Description: Grand Cru volcanic Riesling. Smoky, complex, age-worthy. One of France's finest whites.
Opulent apricot and honeysuckle, full-bodied but fresh. Extraordinary with Thai or Indian spice.
The most mythical CdP. Ethereal and silky, raspberry and garrigue. Limited allocation.
Northern Rhône Syrah at an accessible price. Violet, smoked meat, olive tapenade.
Rich and plummy Late Bottled Vintage. Chocolate, dried fig. No decanting required.
First Growth Sauternes. Apricot jam, crème brûlée, Botrytis complexity. Pair with foie gras.
